_doc: Labeled eval set for /classify_tasks tuning. Balanced so a degenerate always-abstain OR always-match policy both score poorly. report=='FIXTURE' loads the real captured 10.5k-char tracing hour. Other reports are realistic synthetic distillations in the same markdown style. expected = the set of task_keys the hour DEMONSTRABLY advanced (empty = abstain).

KAN-241.title: Emit PM worklog logs to OpenObserve and set up proper debug tracing
KAN-241.doc: [Task] Emit PM worklog logs to OpenObserve and set up proper debug tracing. Epic: Observability & Log Pipeline. Goal: full debug visibility into the PM worklog synthesis pipeline (services/agents) via spans + logs exported to OpenObserve.

KAN-244.title: Improve PM worklog update accuracy
KAN-244.doc: [Task] Improve PM worklog update accuracy. Epic: Accuracy & Automated Deep-Eval. Context: worklogs and observability already exist; this ticket is about raising the correctness of which tickets get matched and what the worklog text says, measured by an eval harness.

KAN-201.title: Add Trello OAuth integration
KAN-201.doc: [Task] Add Trello OAuth integration. Epic: Integrations. Goal: let users connect a Trello board via OAuth (fragment relay + token exchange) so tasks and worklogs sync to Trello.

KAN-210.title: Build the daily-plan /plan page
KAN-210.doc: [Task] Build the daily-plan /plan page. Epic: UI. Goal: a page where the developer confirms today's planned tickets; confirming locks the plan and boosts those tickets in the classifier.

KAN-227.title: Fix screen capture on Electron apps (a11y tree invisible)
KAN-227.doc: [Task] Fix screen capture on Electron apps. Epic: Capture. Electron/Chromium apps are invisible to the macOS accessibility tree; capture must fall back to CGWindowList/OCR for those windows.

cases.note: true match + hard same-topic decoy (KAN-244) must be resisted
find the needle among 3 unrelated tickets
positive on different content; KAN-241/244 are unrelated here
real coding work, but on none of these tickets
read/discussed the ticket but wrote no code — mention is not advancement
only the decoy offered; must abstain rather than stretch to fit

reports.R_OAUTH:
### TLDR
The developer implemented the Trello OAuth connection flow end to end so a user can link their Trello board. They built the fragment-relay callback page, wired the token exchange, and persisted the credentials, then verified a board's lists came back over the API.

### Core Tasks & Projects
**Trello OAuth flow**. The developer wrote the OAuth authorize redirect and a small fragment-relay HTML page that captures the `#token=` fragment Trello returns and posts it back to the daemon. They implemented the token-exchange handler, stored the resulting token in the settings store, and added a `provider=trello` branch to the task-fetch path.
(Why: users could not connect Trello at all before this) (How: fragment relay because Trello returns the token in the URL fragment, not a query param).

**Verification**. They ran the connect flow against a real Trello account, confirmed the callback captured the token, and saw the board's lists and cards returned by the fetch call. Fixed one bug where the token was URL-encoded twice.

### Key Decisions
* Chose a fragment-relay page over a server redirect because Trello delivers the OAuth token in the URL fragment, which never reaches the server.

### Resources Consulted
* Trello REST API OAuth docs; developer.atlassian Trello authorization page.
reports.R_ADMIN:
### TLDR
The developer spent the hour on team administration and communication: a standup video call, triaging email, filing an expense report, and browsing the Jira board without editing any tickets or code.

### Core Tasks & Projects
**Standup & coordination**. The developer joined the daily standup on Google Meet for ~25 minutes, discussing sprint progress and blockers with the team. They took notes in a scratch doc.
(How: Google Meet; notes in Notion).

**Email & admin**. They worked through the inbox — replying to a recruiter, forwarding a vendor invoice, and submitting last week's expense report in the finance portal. They also skimmed the Jira board to see ticket statuses but made no edits.

### Key Decisions
* None of note.

### Resources Consulted
* Gmail, Google Meet, the company finance/expense portal, the Jira board (read-only).
reports.R_UI:
### TLDR
The developer fixed visual layout bugs on the Settings page — a misaligned card grid and a broken dark-mode toggle — entirely in the React/CSS front end. No backend, pipeline, capture, or integration work.

### Core Tasks & Projects
**Settings page layout**. The developer reworked the CSS grid on the Settings page so the cards no longer overflowed on narrow windows, switching to a responsive `minmax` track. They adjusted padding and fixed a z-index issue where a tooltip rendered behind a card.
(Why: the settings cards overlapped on small windows) (How: CSS grid `minmax`, z-index fix in SettingsView.tsx).

**Dark-mode toggle**. They fixed the dark-mode switch which was not persisting the user's choice, wiring it to localStorage and the root `data-theme` attribute. Verified the theme survived a reload.

### Key Decisions
* Kept the theme in localStorage rather than the settings DB to avoid a round-trip on every paint.

### Resources Consulted
* MDN CSS grid docs; the project's existing SettingsView.tsx and theme.css.
reports.R_MENTION:
### TLDR
The developer spent the hour reading and discussing the observability ticket but did not write or change any code. They opened KAN-241 in Jira, read the OpenObserve tracing requirements, and talked through the approach in a call, deferring the implementation.

### Core Tasks & Projects
**Reviewing the tracing ticket**. The developer opened KAN-241 ("Emit PM worklog logs to OpenObserve and set up proper debug tracing") and read through its description and acceptance criteria. They browsed the OpenObserve docs to understand what would be involved but did not start coding.
(How: read the Jira ticket and OpenObserve docs in the browser).

**Planning discussion**. They joined a call with a teammate to discuss how the tracing work would be sequenced relative to other tickets, and agreed to pick it up tomorrow. No branch was created and no files were edited.

### Key Decisions
* Decided to defer the tracing implementation to the next day.

### Resources Consulted
* Jira ticket KAN-241 (read-only); OpenObserve documentation; a planning call.
